blockingIssues.description: The older-history load-more catch path still renders raw Error.message text, so users can still see backend or generic failure copy instead of the required explicit Leagueback wording.
The match-details header still exposes implementation-facing cache/source labels such as 'Cached match data' and 'Legacy cached match data' instead of intentional product-facing wording.
The fix commit only changed the non-Error fallback branch for older-history loading, so raw Error.message text can still surface to users on that path.
The fix commit still leaves implementation-facing cache/source labels visible in the details header, so the prior scrutiny finding remains unresolved.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in the web-feature-worker skill whether copy-only UI polish work may rely on source review alone or must still capture browser/dev-server runtime evidence when agent-browser is unavailable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed review for web-copy-and-empty-state-polish noted that the worker handoff only cited source review for runtime verification, leaving expectations for copy-only polish validation ambiguous under the current fallback guidance.
